findings.issue: Phase 9 claims all 7 audit passes completed successfully, but manifest-based structural comparison for D8 MVP build cards against D3 feature registry is incomplete—no verification that every MVP feature ID in D8 appears in D3's authoritative list.
findings.evidence: Section 2 states "Pass 1 (Name Consistency): Verified — zero naming conflicts across 20 deliverables" and claims all 12 MVP features verified, but the actual cross-check table in Pass 1 does not show explicit line-by-line comparison of D8 `feature_mappings` against D3 `mvp_features` list; only narrative assertion provided.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit table in Pass 1 showing D8 build card feature IDs (F-001 through F-012) matched against D3 MVP feature registry, confirming zero orphaned cards.

findings.issue: Table lists "D2: refund_policy" manifest update claiming "Non-refundable after ANY course confirms" but D2 user persona in Phase 2 deliverable is not loaded, and Phase 9 cannot validate this decision against the original D2 specification without loading it.
findings.evidence: Section 0.5 references D2 manifest changes but only one side of the decision (the outcome) is visible; no evidence that the original D2 refund policy was different or that this change was necessary to close a Phase 9 gap.
findings.recommendation: Load D2 Section 1 (User Roles & Decision Authority) to verify refund policy as originally specified, then document whether Phase 9 identified a contradiction that needed resolving or whether this was founder clarification recorded retroactively.

findings.issue: Name consistency check claims 10 entity types validated as consistent, but the table shows only 9 items (Features, Roles, Tiers, Course Count, Magic Link, Revenue Streams, Payment Service, Email Service, Database); the 10th is missing from the enumeration despite the text claiming "10 entity types checked."
findings.evidence: Pass 1 name consistency table has 9 rows; text says "10 entity types checked, all consistent." Discrepancy between stated count and actual table rows.
findings.recommendation: Either add the missing 10th entity type to the table or correct the text to say "9 entity types."

findings.issue: Clarification #3 (Lucia's Reschedule Authority) claims "D4 Section 1 — booking_modifications table extended for audit trail" but does not show evidence that D4's original booking_modifications table definition (from Phase 4) lacked these audit fields or that they were added during Phase 9 validation.
findings.evidence: The manifest corrections table (Section 0.5) lists booking_modifications table changes, but the detailed resolution in Clarification #3 does not explain whether this was a discovery (D4 was incomplete) or retroactive documentation (table already existed as designed).
findings.recommendation: Clarify whether D4's original booking_modifications table included (modification_reason, modified_by, old_course_id, new_course_id, modification_reason) columns as specified in Phase 4, or whether these were discovered missing during Phase 9 validation and added retroactively.

findings.section: Section 3: Research Findings — Competitive Landscape Refresh
findings.issue: Research point 9.2 (competitive landscape verification) claims findings verified as of "April 1, 2026, 14:30 UTC" but the Phase 9 validation report does not document whether this research was executed by the AI during Phase 9 synthesis or assumed from prior research; no ambient research execution log provided.
findings.evidence: Section 3 reports findings but contains no methodology note indicating Perplexity Sonar was executed, no search query provided, no source citations for individual findings (e.g., "GolfNow still marketplace-only" — from what source?), no timestamp confirmation of live research execution.
findings.recommendation: Add ambient research execution section documenting Perplexity Sonar query, timestamp, and source citations for each finding (GolfNow status, TravelGolf offerings, Fore Club pricing, new entrants). If research was not actually executed, note it as "assumed from Phase 1 baseline" and recommend Phase 10 live verification.

findings.section: Section 4: Issues Ledger → Issue 1 (Magic Link Resend Logic)
findings.issue: Issue 1 resolution claims "Dashboard indicator in F-002 (Rafael's Request Dashboard). Rafael manually initiates resends. No background job required for MVP." but D8 Week 6 build cards are not loaded to verify that FC-024 (F-002 build card) actually includes the "link status indicator" acceptance criteria or that background job work was explicitly not assigned.
findings.evidence: The issue resolution narrative describes the decision but does not cite the specific D8 build card that implements this, nor does it reference the acceptance criteria in that card to confirm it includes "link status indicator with color coding."
findings.recommendation: Load D8 FC-024 (or whichever card builds F-002 dashboard) and verify the acceptance criteria include link expiration countdown display; cite the specific card and acceptance criteria in the issue resolution.

findings.section: Section 8: Phase 9 Completion Status → Manifest-Based Audit Completeness
findings.issue: Gate item states "Reference resolution complete: D20 outbound references verified, all 20 reference checks, 0 broken links" but Section 6 (Cross-Reference Verification Table) only shows 10 references verified (D1→D2, D1→D3, etc.); claim of "all 20" reference checks is not substantiated by the table provided.
findings.evidence: Section 6 table shows 10 rows of reference pairs (D1→D2, D1→D3, ... D10→D19); completion gate claims "20 reference checks" but table shows only 10. Discrepancy between claimed scope and visible evidence.
findings.recommendation: Either expand Section 6 table to show all 20 reference checks actually performed, or update completion gate to state accurate count (10 reference pairs verified). Clarify whether "20 reference checks" means 20 individual references or 10 bidirectional pairs (20 directions).

findings.issue: Launch Day Gap Checklist claims "NONE. All items required for launch day are covered" but does not verify that Phase 7 build will produce all required components by June 10, nor does it cross-check against D64 (Phase 7 Build Team Briefing, which is referenced in Phase 7 template as containing build timeline) to confirm build cards will actually deliver all these items.
findings.evidence: Section 7 lists 11 launch day requirements and checks them off as ✅, but the verification method is not explained: Are these items in D8 build cards? Is D64 confirming these are in the 10-week timeline? No reference to upstream build plan provided.
findings.recommendation: Load D8 build phase summary (or D64 if available) and verify that FC cards corresponding to each checklist item are scheduled and that their acceptance criteria cover the launch day requirement. Create explicit traceability: each ✅ item → corresponding FC card(s) → acceptance criteria.

findings.section: Section 1: Structural Audit Methodology → Pass 5 (Same-Phase Cross-Deliverable Consistency)
findings.issue: D-59 accessibility chain validation claims "D14 validation (all from manifests): D14 manifest exists" but the actual validation narrative in the subsection checks 5 items (D14 exists, components_covered matches, source_sections includes D7 references, D8 build cards reference D14, D20 includes accessibility maintenance) without explaining why these 5 specific checks constitute "D-59 chain of custody" or how they relate to the D-59 protocol referenced in the phase template.
findings.evidence: Section 5 states "This check was previously only in Phase 1E's pre-flight — extending it to Phase 9 catches contradictions introduced in later phases" but does not explain what Phase 1E's accessibility validation was or why these 5 checks are the correct extension of that logic.
findings.recommendation: Add one-sentence explanation linking each of the 5 D-59 checks to the original Phase 1E pre-flight logic (e.g., "Check #1 verifies deliverable exists because Phase 1E baseline required D14 to exist" or similar), making the chain of custody logic transparent.

findings.section: Section 1: Structural Audit Methodology → Pass 2 (Value Consistency Check)
findings.issue: Pass 2 claims "Magic link validity: D2 says 72 hours, D4 says 72 hours, D6 says 72 hours ✅ CONSISTENT" but does not reference or check D8 build cards (FC cards that implement magic link feature) or D16 analytics (which may need to track link expiration events) to verify these downstream specifications also assume 72-hour validity without drift.
findings.evidence: Pass 2 validates values across D2/D4/D6 but stops there; no evidence that the value was checked in D8 build cards or D16 event definitions, which might reference the link expiration window in their specifications.
findings.recommendation: Extend Pass 2 value consistency checks to include downstream deliverables where the value is consequential (e.g., 72-hour magic link expiration implies: D8 Week 3 build card must handle 72-hour token, D16 must track link expiration event, D17 must have 72-hour reminder email). Spot-check at least 3 critical values across 4+ deliverables each.

findings.section: Section 4: Issues Ledger → Issue 1, Issue 2, Issue 3
findings.issue: Each of the three "resolved" issues documents the decision outcome but does not explain the ROOT CAUSE discovery method: How did Phase 9 identify these gaps? Did manifest comparison reveal them, or were they pre-existing decisions from the decision ledger that Phase 9 is retroactively documenting?
findings.evidence: Issue 1 states "Dashboard indicator in F-002" as resolution but does not explain whether Phase 9 discovered the gap by comparing D2 manifest (missing monitoring method) against D8 build cards (D8 does not reference dashboard indicator), or whether this was a founder clarification from Phase 8 that Phase 9 is documenting.
findings.recommendation: For each issue, add one sentence explaining the discovery method: "Phase 9 discovered this gap by comparing [manifest X field] against [manifest Y field]" or "This gap was flagged in Phase 8 decision ledger entry D55-P8-005; Phase 9 verified the correction was propagated to all downstream manifests."

findings.section: Completion Gate: All 7 Audit Passes Checklist
findings.issue: Completion gate item "Reference resolution complete: D20 outbound references verified" does not specify what "verified" means: Were references hand-checked for existence, or was an automated tool used? Were all 20+ references checked, or a sample? No audit methodology described.
findings.evidence: Completion gate lists "✅ Reference resolution complete" but preceding Section 6 shows only 10 reference pairs in the table; unclear if all possible cross-references were checked or only a sample.
findings.recommendation: Add note to completion gate: "20+ cross-references audited via manifest field inspection (references_out matched against target deliverables' sections map); method: [automated cross-check / manual sample of X% / full enumeration]. Sample size: Y references checked, Z% coverage."

findings.issue: Table shows "Status: ✅ VALID" for all 10 reference pairs but does not explain what "VALID" means: Does it mean reference resolves to an existing section, or does it mean the reference is used correctly in context? E.g., D1 → D2 "Status: ✅ VALID" could mean "D1 references D2 and D2 exists" OR "D1's revenue model constraints reference D2's user personas and the personas are used to derive constraints."
findings.evidence: Table defines validity only as "reference resolves to actual content" in the introductory text but does not explain semantic validity (is the reference used correctly, or just present?).
findings.recommendation: Add definition: "VALID = reference resolves to existing section in target deliverable AND semantic context is appropriate (e.g., D1→D3 constraint reference matches D3 feature dependency). Semantic spot-checks performed on 3 critical references [specify which 3]; remainder checked for existence only."

findings.issue: Certification statement claims "✅ Holistic product completeness confirmed" but does not reference the specific method used to confirm completeness: Was it the launch day gap checklist (Section 7), or the service/feature chains validation (Holistic Completeness section)? Different methods could yield different conclusions.
findings.evidence: Certification lists 8 checkmarks (structural consistency, name consistency, value consistency, etc.) but the methodology for "holistic completeness" is not named; Section 7 provides a gap checklist but the connection to the certification statement is implicit.
findings.recommendation: Change certification to cite specific method: "✅ Holistic product completeness confirmed via Launch Day Gap Checklist (Section 7) and Service/Feature Chain Validation showing [N] complete chains out of [N] required."

findings.issue: Table lists D2 manifest updates for "lucia_authority" and refund policy but does not show the corresponding updates to D17 (Email System, which affects Lucia's workflow) or D20 (Post-Launch Maintenance, which may reference Lucia's autonomous authority). Unclear whether these downstream impacts were validated.
findings.evidence: Section 0.5 shows updates to D2, D4, D6, D8, D10 but does not mention whether D17 (email templates for rescheduling notification) or D20 (Lucia's procedures) were checked for consistency with the Lucia authority clarifications.
findings.recommendation: Add note: "Lucia authority clarifications propagated to: D2 (definition), D4 (schema), D6 (API), D8 (build cards), D17 (email for booking_rescheduled template), D20 (procedures for reschedule escalation)." Verify all downstream references in manifests show consistent understanding of autonomous authority scope.

findings.section: Section 1: Structural Audit Methodology → Pass 7 (Holistic Completeness Check)
findings.issue: Holistic completeness check includes semantic specification drift sampling for 3 MVP features (F-001, F-007, F-010) but does not explain selection criteria: Why these 3? Are they the highest-risk features, or randomly selected? No power analysis provided to justify sample size.
findings.evidence: Subsection 7 states "Select 3 of the most complex components" but does not define "most complex" or explain why 3 is sufficient to validate semantic fidelity across 12 MVP features.
findings.recommendation: Add note: "3 features selected via risk-based sampling: F-001 (database model complexity), F-007 (multi-table coordination), F-010 (third-party integration). Sample justification: features touching most deliverables (>4 each). Power: 3/12 = 25% coverage. If all 3 pass, semantic drift is low confidence (<50% coverage recommended for higher assurance)."
